title: Network Security, Attacks, Segmentation, and Hardening · Independent Network Trial
summary: Threat-model and harden enterprise, guest, IoT, OT, management, and remote-access network zones. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a defense-in-depth network security plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish confidentiality, integrity, availability, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, likelihood, and impact.
Protect data in transit and at rest and explain certificates, PKI trust, self-signed limits, key management, and rotation.
Use MFA, SSO, RADIUS, LDAP, SAML, TACACS+, time-based authentication, authorization, RBAC, and least privilege appropriately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Zero Trust Architecture — Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

hints: Name the Net Acl Filtering precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed lab case for network security that tests this competency: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.. Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An ACL automatically evaluates the most specific rule before earlier rules.

stimulus.title: Net Acl Filtering failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An ACL automatically evaluates the most specific rule before earlier rules.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Net Acl Filtering without its stated preconditions, omit the a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Net Acl Filtering for a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Net Acl Filtering condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Net Acl Filtering output and hide the violated a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Net Acl Filtering: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Use a changed lab case for network security that tests this competency: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.. steps.why: Retrieving Net Acl Filtering through the concrete a complete first version of a defense-in-depth network security plan while every earlier competency remains active case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design ordered ACL, URL, and content filtering rules with least privilege, state, direction, logging, and exception review.
